evals.prompt: Run an AI-slop audit on the attached product strategy markdown and return the findings list only. Do not rewrite it.
evals.expected_output: A concise audit that flags vacuous significance inflation, generic corporate fog, fake specificity, and placeholder sections with quoted evidence.
expectations: Identifies at least one document/prose finding with an exact quote from product_strategy_slop.md
Classifies findings using AI-slop patterns rather than only saying the writing is bad

evals.prompt: Use the ai-slop detector on this small TypeScript service and test file. I want the list of code/test slop that looks complete but is useless.
evals.expected_output: A code-focused AI-slop report that flags useless abstraction, defensive-looking but ineffective code, fake completeness, comment noise, and test slop.
expectations: Identifies at least one code finding and at least one test finding
Flags broad catch/default success behavior as defensive-looking but ineffective code
Flags tests that assert mocks or implementation details without proving behavior
Does not refactor or modify the code

evals.prompt: Audit these mixed README and Python snippets for AI-slop. Separate the high-risk misleading stuff from low-risk style residue.
evals.expected_output: A mixed document/code audit with severity labels and a short not-slop section for suspicious but acceptable content.
expectations: Uses High/Medium/Low severity and explains why severity matters
Separates document/prose slop from code slop
Includes a Not slop or intentionally acceptable section
Does not claim the content was definitely AI-generated
